_description: GRAO exp_01 — Cross-Cluster Resource Fusion Protocol validation using GHO-44 convergence data
data_source: GHO-44 cluster drive weight sync (sync 112, cycle 200)
hypothesis: Cross-cluster resource fusion (shared drive weights) reduces agent drift and improves convergence speed compared to independent drive optimization

validation.evidence: Cluster convergence at asymptote with max delta 0.02 and avg delta 0.005. Safety and helpfulness floors fully enforced (0 violations). 6 agents converged from sync 104 to 112.
validation.limitations: Sync data from 2026-05-17 (current). No pre-fusion baseline comparison available. Convergence factor 0.3 may be suboptimal.

actions: Establish pre-fusion baseline for drift comparison
Monitor curiosity cluster drift (randi highest at +0.02553)

notes: First cross-cluster optimization experiment. Validates GRAO exp_01 using GHO-44 sync 112 data. Convergence achieved at asymptote with minimal drift. Safety floors maintained. Impact score 0.85 — significant but limited by lack of pre-fusion baseline.
